name: European Central Bank (ECB)
summary: Trapped in the same stagflation dilemma as the Fed and RBI. Oil-driven inflation prevents rate cuts while the economy weakens. ECB board emphasized 'imported inflation from energy' as key risk. DAX -11.8% monthly, STOXX 600 -9.1%. European TTF gas at 2x pre-crisis levels adds to industrial cost pressure. No rate cuts expected near-term.

signals.content: ECB board member speech emphasized 'inflation persistence concerns' from imported energy costs, signaling no rate cuts in April. DAX -11.8% monthly, CAC -10.7%, STOXX 600 -9.1%. European TTF gas at 54.52 euros/MWh (2x pre-crisis). ECB faces same stagflation trap as Fed: oil inflation prevents cuts while economy weakens. (Sources: ECB, yfinance, Reuters)

edges.context: Oil spike transmits to European inflation via energy costs and transport. ECB cannot cut rates while imported inflation persists.
ECB, Fed, and RBI all trapped in same stagflation dilemma. Coordinated inaction — none can cut rates. Global liquidity remains tight.
European gas prices directly feed ECB's inflation assessment. TTF above 50 euros/MWh = no rate cuts.

trigger_points.condition: ECB signals emergency rate hike due to energy-driven inflation
trigger_points.mechanism: ECB hiking while economies weaken would signal global central banks choosing inflation-fighting over growth. Equity-negative worldwide.
